what_it_does: A scheduled beat script that keeps a searchable substrate index current for exported Claude session markdown. It first performs a cheap filesystem-level change check against a stored marker, then runs a delta reindex only when transcripts have changed, and fails loudly if the embedder is unavailable and work is needed.
observations: Uses a marker file (.reindex_marker.json) in the corpus directory to remember the newest .md mtime and file count from the last successful index.
Compares corpus mtimes and file counts before touching any GPU, model, or database.
Checks embedder health via an HTTP health endpoint (default http://127.0.0.1:8007/health).
Optionally brings the embedder up via the company CLI (`company ensure embed-pplx`) only when --ensure-embedder is passed.
Performs a delta reindex by importing wire_substrate_claude_sessions and substrate_mcp modules and calling scanner_mod.scan_vault with embed_now=True.
On first run (no marker) it performs an initial full build.
Refuses to advance the marker if the resulting chroma vector count is zero.
Leaves the marker stale on any failure so the next timer fire retries.
Uses print for all status and error output; no logging module.
Inserts ops/ into sys.path to import sibling modules.
standouts: The cheap-by-design marker check prevents unnecessary GPU/embedder work in the common case.
Explicit fail-loud semantics: errors and missing embedder leave the marker stale for retry rather than silently skipping.
Optional --ensure-embedder delegates embedder lifecycle to the resource manager only when explicitly requested.
Delta reindex relies on sha256-based unchanged-file skipping inside the substrate scanner.
conventions: Heavy module-level docstring explaining purpose, wiring, and design rationale.
Uses argparse, pathlib, urllib.request, subprocess, and json from the standard library.
Configuration is overridable via environment variables (CLAUDE_SESSIONS_CORPUS, PPLX_EMBED_HEALTH).
Marker is written atomically via a .tmp file and os.replace.
Imports sibling modules by mutating sys.path rather than package imports.
Broad exception handling at the top level to ensure failure is reported and the marker stays stale.
concerns: Catches a broad Exception around run_delta_reindex, which could mask different failure modes.
Relies on internal-looking functions of wire_substrate_claude_sessions (cmd_setup, _load_cfg) whose contracts are not visible in this file.
Hardcodes relative paths and default ports/URLs that may drift with deployment changes.
Imports heavy modules inside run_delta_reindex, making failure modes less obvious from the top of the file.
ensure_embedder uses a fixed 40-iteration loop with 3-second sleeps, which may be brittle for slow embedder startup.
The marker is based only on file count and newest mtime, not content hashes, so identical rewrites could still trigger reindex.
notes: A lean, marker-gated reindex beat that closes the loop between Claude session export and searchable substrate index while refusing to waste GPU cycles.
questions: What exact interface does wire_substrate_claude_sessions expose (cmd_setup, _load_cfg, VAULT)?
What fields and types does scanner_mod.scan_vault return in its report object?
How does `company ensure embed-pplx` behave when the embedder is already up, and how long can it realistically take?
What is the expected JSON schema returned by the PPLX_EMBED_HEALTH endpoint?
Are multiple concurrent runs of this beat (timer + manual) safe against the same marker and Chroma store?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a separate, cheap, delta reindex beat that only runs when exports change, fails loudly, and optionally ensures the embedder.
apparent_themes: Cheap common path / guard expensive work with a cheap pre-check.
Fail loud and retry by leaving state stale.
Separate lifecycle concerns: export, indexing, and embedder management are distinct stages.
Delta over full work whenever possible.
Observability through explicit printed status messages.
intention_signals: The file is trying to automate the last mile of a transcript-to-search pipeline: detect when new Claude session markdown exists, gate on embedder readiness, and update the vector index with minimal redundant work, while making failures visible and recoverable.
novelty: It is explicitly labeled a throwaway interim tool but implements production-like behavior: a marker-gated, delta-only, GPU-avoiding reindex beat with optional resource-manager integration and loud failure semantics.
contribution: Provides a scheduled, marker-gated delta reindex step that bridges session export and searchable index, including embedder health gating, optional embedder bring-up, and stale-marker retry semantics.
summary_for_embedding: ops/claude_sessions_reindex.py is a Python beat that scans a Claude session markdown corpus, compares it against a local marker, and runs a delta substrate reindex only when exports have changed, with optional embedder management and loud failure handling.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to keep the claude-sessions search index current after periodic markdown exports without spinning up the embedder when nothing has changed.

symbols.does: Computes the newest .md mtime and total markdown file count in the corpus tree.
symbols.description: Walks CORPUS.rglob('*.md') using stat() only; returns a dict with newest_mtime and n_files. This is the cheap signal used to decide whether an expensive reindex is needed.

symbols.does: Reads the persisted .reindex_marker.json if it exists and is valid JSON.
symbols.description: Attempts to parse MARKER as JSON; returns an empty dict on any OSError or JSON decode error, which causes the next run to treat the state as a first run.

symbols.does: Writes the current corpus signature plus result metadata to the marker file atomically.
symbols.description: Builds a dict from the signature, adds indexed_at and optional extra fields, writes to a temporary file, and replaces the marker atomically so a successful index is recorded.

symbols.does: Compares the current corpus signature with the stored marker and returns a decision plus reason.
symbols.description: Returns (True, reason) when there is no marker, file count differs, or the newest mtime is newer than the marker's by more than a small epsilon; otherwise returns (False, 'nothing changed since last index').

symbols.does: Probes the configured embedder health endpoint and checks for an 'ok' status.
symbols.description: Performs a GET to PPLX_EMBED_HEALTH with a 5-second timeout and parses the JSON response; returns True only if status equals 'ok', otherwise returns False.

symbols.does: Optionally asks the resource manager to bring the embedder up and waits for it to become healthy.
symbols.description: Runs `company ensure embed-pplx` via subprocess, streams its output, then polls embedder_up for up to 40 iterations with 3-second sleeps; returns True if the embedder ends up healthy.

symbols.does: Executes a substrate delta scan of the claude-sessions vault and returns a summary report.
symbols.description: Imports the wire and substrate modules, ensures wiring is set up, loads config, connects the database, creates an embedder and Chroma client, then calls scanner_mod.scan_vault in delta mode. Collects file, chunk, and vector counts and returns them in a dict.

symbols.does: Entry point that parses arguments, decides whether to reindex, gates on embedder health, runs the delta reindex, and updates the marker on success.
symbols.description: Parses --check, --force, and --ensure-embedder flags; computes the corpus signature; prints the decision; exits early on --check or when no change is detected; requires the embedder to be up (or brings it up if requested); runs the delta reindex; advances the marker only if vectors were produced; and returns non-zero on failure.
